title: China Could Win Taiwan Without Fighting
gist: This is a paywall-truncated excerpt of an essay by Richard Haass and David Sacks (full argument on Trump's Taiwan equivocation not visible in the collected text); the visible portion establishes that Washington and Beijing both call theirs the world's most important bilateral relationship and both agree Taiwan is the likeliest trigger for war between them, and that the Trump-Xi Beijing summit, despite an extended Taiwan discussion, was 'anything but reassuring' and represented what the authors call Xi's opening move in a longer campaign.

insights.claim: The U.S. and Chinese governments both describe their bilateral relationship as the world's most important, and both sides agree that Taiwan is the likeliest trigger for a direct armed conflict between them.
insights.speaker: Richard N. Haass
insights.so_what: Confirms Taiwan remains the consensus central flashpoint for U.S.-China escalation risk that investors in the region should continue to monitor above other bilateral frictions.
insights.quote: the likeliest trigger for an armed conflict between them is Taiwan
insights.locator: The governments of the United States and China both assert

insights.claim: Donald Trump and Xi Jinping held an extended discussion of Taiwan during their May 2026 summit in Beijing, which in theory left the two leaders with a better mutual understanding of each other's positions on the issue.
insights.speaker: Richard N. Haass
insights.so_what: Dates and locates the specific diplomatic event (Beijing, May 2026) that the authors treat as the pivot point for their subsequent, more critical argument.
insights.locator: U.S. President Donald Trump and Chinese leader Xi Jinping discussed Taiwan at length in Beijing

insights.claim: Contrary to the hope that the Trump-Xi Beijing summit would ease Taiwan tensions, the authors characterize it as 'anything but reassuring,' framing it instead as the opening move in what they describe as a longer Chinese campaign.
insights.speaker: Richard N. Haass
insights.so_what: A contrarian read against the surface optics of the summit — signals the authors see the summit as escalatory groundwork by Beijing rather than de-escalation, worth weighing against consensus relief narratives.
insights.locator: Yet the U.S.-Chinese summit was anything but reassuring
